### Post by SpinningAround on 2007-10-26
While in ubuntu do I get sound but when I playing a movie on the tv do I get sound from the computer but not from the tv, I'm using a s-video cable. Using Mplayer as media player.

Xorg.conf
```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Apr 16 20:38:05 PDT 2007

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"

    Option         "Xinerama" "0"

    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "DELL D825TM"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 70.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 120.0

    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: 1024x768 +0+0, TV: 1024x768 +1024+0; CRT: 1280x1024 +0+0, TV: nvidia-auto-select +1277+0; CRT: 1152x864 +0+0, TV: NULL; CRT: 1024x768 +0+0, TV: nvidia-auto-select +0+0; CRT: 832x624 +0+0, TV: 800x600 +0+0; CRT: 800x600 +0+0, TV: 720x480 +0+0; CRT: 720x400 +0+0, TV: 640x480 +0+0; CRT: 640x480 +0+0, TV: 400x300 +0+0; TV: 320x200 +0+0"
```

### Post by ddrichardson on 2007-10-26
S-Video doesn't carry sound.

---

### Post by SpinningAround on 2007-10-26
ah :) 

Still I tested with the RCA (?) connector and connected it to the green input still nothing happend.

The tv is PAL and it's running at the channel SVHC2, the EXT only show gray scale

---

### Post by ddrichardson on 2007-10-26
If you are using RCA to connect the computer to the TV, the problem wont lie in X but in the ALSA driver, check that the headphone jack is not muted first - some soundcards have problems with the headphone jack too.

---
